% ===========================================================================
% §1  Candidate Under Review
% ===========================================================================

\section{Candidate Under Review}

\begin{resultbox}[Candidate Summary]
\textbf{Candidate ID:} \texttt{cand-qho-energy-spectrum}

\textbf{Claim:} The energy eigenvalues of the 1D quantum harmonic oscillator
with Hamiltonian $H = p^2/(2m) + \frac{1}{2} m \omega^2 x^2$ are
$E_n = (n + 1/2) \hbar \omega$, for $n = 0, 1, 2, \ldots$

\textbf{Regime of Validity:} Non-relativistic QM, 1D, harmonic potential only.

\textbf{Type:} \texttt{result} \quad
\textbf{Lane:} \texttt{formal\_theory}
\end{resultbox}

% ===========================================================================
% §2  L4 Validation Outcome
% ===========================================================================

\section{L4 Validation Outcome}

\subsection{Primary Review (Cycle 1)}

\begin{valbox}[Review: cand-qho-energy-spectrum]
\textbf{Outcome:} \statuspass{} \texttt{pass}

\textbf{Review Date:} 2026-04-20T12:00:00Z

\textbf{Notes:} All four mandatory physics checks passed. The derivation
correctly recovers the well-known quantum harmonic oscillator energy spectrum.
The ladder operator method is a standard technique validated across multiple
textbooks.
\end{valbox}

\subsection{Physics Check Results}

\begin{longtable}{>{\raggedright}p{4cm} p{2cm} p{6cm}}
\toprule
\textbf{Check} & \textbf{Result} & \textbf{Detail} \\
\midrule
\endhead
Dimensional consistency & \statuspass & $[H] = [\hbar\omega] = \text{energy}$, both sides $ML^2T^{-2}$ \\
Symmetry compatibility & \statuspass & $H$ commutes with parity operator $\hat{P}$ \\
Limiting case check  & \statuspass & Classical HO recovered as $n \to \infty$: $T = 2\pi/\omega$ \\
Correspondence check & \statuspass & Matches known result $E_n = (n+1/2)\hbar\omega$ (Griffiths \S2.3) \\
\bottomrule
\end{longtable}

\subsection{Devil's Advocate}

\begin{quote}
The derivation assumes the potential is exactly harmonic. Anharmonic
corrections ($x^3$, $x^4$ terms) would shift the spectrum. Regime boundary:
coupling must be weak enough that perturbation theory converges.
\end{quote}

\subsection{SymPy Verification Evidence}

\textbf{Tool:} \texttt{aitp\_verify\_dimensions}

\begin{verbatim}
{
  "pass": true,
  "lhs_dimension": [1, 2, -2, 0, 0],
  "rhs_dimension": [1, 2, -2, 0, 0]
}
\end{verbatim}

% §3  Versioned Review (Cycle 2)

\section{Versioned Review (Cycle 2)}

\begin{valbox}[Review: cand-qho-energy-spectrum\_v2]
\textbf{Outcome:} \statuspass{} \texttt{pass}

\textbf{Review Date:} 2026-04-21T09:00:00Z

\textbf{Notes:} Second review cycle after addressing v1 feedback on anharmonic
corrections. Confirmed that the harmonic spectrum is correct in the
weak-coupling limit. Anharmonic corrections estimated at $O(\lambda)$ with
$\lambda \ll 1$.
\end{valbox}

\subsection{Updated Check Results (v2)}

\begin{longtable}{>{\raggedright}p{4cm} p{2cm} p{6cm}}
\toprule
\textbf{Check} & \textbf{Result} & \textbf{Detail} \\
\midrule
\endhead
Dimensional consistency & \statuspass & Reconfirmed after anharmonic correction analysis \\
Symmetry compatibility & \statuspass & Parity confirmed even with $x^4$ perturbation \\
Limiting case check  & \statuspass & Classical HO period $T = 2\pi/\omega$ recovered \\
Correspondence check & \statuspass & Matches known result after v1 corrections \\
\bottomrule
\end{longtable}

\subsection{Devil's Advocate (v2)}

\begin{quote}
The anharmonic $x^4$ correction shifts $E_0$ by $\sim 0.01\,\hbar\omega$
at weak coupling. Stronger coupling would break the harmonic approximation
entirely.
\end{quote}
